overview: Midjourney speaks in nouns and adjectives, not sentences. The r/midjourney community has established that style references (artist names, art movements, film stocks) do more work than descriptive sentences. Parameters are first-class — --ar, --s, --chaos are not optional. Think of it as tagging a concept, not writing a brief.

style_reference.description: Naming a photographer, painter, director, or art movement is the single highest-leverage lever in Midjourney. 'by Gregory Crewdson' changes everything about the output.

aspect_ratio.description: Without --ar, MJ defaults to 1:1. Always set it. Composition changes completely with ratio.

lighting.description: Lighting terms in MJ are short keywords, not sentences. Comma-separate them.

keyword_format.description: Comma-separated descriptive terms outperform full sentences in MJ. The model treats commas as concept separators.

parameters.description: MJ-native parameters that control output style and quality.

power_patterns: Portrait of [subject], [lighting], [color palette], by [photographer], shot on [film/camera], --ar 4:5 --s 500
antipatterns: Full sentences — 'I want to create an image of a woman standing in a forest' → should be: 'woman, ancient forest, dappled light, by Ansel Adams, --ar 3:2'
Conversational language — 'please make', 'I want', 'can you' — MJ ignores these entirely
Too many competing styles — 2+ artist names fight each other. Pick one.
No --ar — you're leaving composition to chance.
Generic color terms — 'colorful', 'vibrant' — instead: 'teal and orange color grade', 'warm golden palette', 'desaturated with cyan tones'

strong: elderly woman, morning market, Hong Kong, candid street photography, natural window light, shot on Leica M6, Kodak Portra 400, by Vivian Maier, --ar 3:2 --s 600
abandoned Victorian greenhouse, overgrown, mist, dramatic shaft of sunlight through broken glass, photorealistic, by Gregory Crewdson, shallow depth of field, --ar 16:9 --s 750 --chaos 10
weak: A beautiful woman standing in a forest with nice colors and good lighting.
Make a cool photo of a city at night.
